title: hyalosperma cotula
intro: Hyalosperma cotula, also known as the New Zealand copper, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its chemical compounds, particularly sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. Hyalosperma cotula can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
